concerns.text: T012 (Data Loader) and T013 (Feature Engineering) are listed as parallel [P] tasks in Phase 3, but T013 explicitly consumes the output of T012 ('Fetch... trigger Synthetic Mode'). T013 cannot execute before T012 completes. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule.

T021 (Model Training) and T024 (Evaluation) are listed as parallel [P] tasks in Phase 4. T024 requires the trained models produced by T021. T024 cannot run before T021. This is a hard ordering violation.

T032 (Statistical Test) and T034 (Sensitivity Analysis) are listed as parallel [P] tasks. T032 requires cross-validated ROC-AUC scores (produced by T024/T025 in Phase 4). T034 also requires model outputs. While T032 and T034 might be parallel to each other, the entire Phase 5 block is marked as dependent on Foundational only, ignoring the strict dependency on Phase 4 (US2) completion. The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes US3 depends on US2, but the task list [P] tags imply US3 tasks can start immediately after Foundational.

T010 and T011 (Tests) are marked [P] and placed before T012-T018. While TDD suggests writing tests first, the task descriptions for T010 ('Contract test for data loader output schema') and T011 ('Integration test for temporal split') imply the existence of the code being tested. If these are 'stub' tests, they are parallel-safe. If they are functional tests, they must follow implementation. The [P] tag is ambiguous here without explicit 'stub' or 'TDD-first' context in the description.
